turns.user_message: I’ve noticed lately that I just stare at my to-do list without actually moving a muscle. It’s like my brain hits a wall the second I try to focus on anything that requires real effort. I used to just power through these slumps with a quick coffee or a walk, but now it feels like I’m wading through thick mud. I keep telling myself it’s just a rough patch and that I’ll bounce back by tomorrow. Honestly, it’s just weird how quickly my usual drive seems to evaporate into thin air.

I keep catching myself completely zoning out during routine tasks that should take five minutes at most. It has been dragging on for months now, and that initial weird feeling has settled into this heavy, constant fog. I find myself reading the same paragraph over and over without absorbing a single word, just waiting for the clock to tick forward. My usual tricks for snapping out of it don’t work anymore, and I feel this quiet frustration bubbling up every single afternoon. I just wish my brain would stop treating simple responsibilities like they are impossible mountains to climb.

I’ve started noticing that my hands actually shake when I open my laptop, and my chest gets tight before I even begin. This heavy numbness has completely taken over, turning every single obligation into a suffocating weight that I can barely carry. I sit there for hours watching the cursor blink while my mind races in frantic, useless circles. The frustration has morphed into this deep, hollow exhaustion that makes me question why I bother trying at all anymore. I just feel completely hollowed out, like I’m running on empty fumes with absolutely nothing left to give.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
